article_title: Meta to Cut 8,000 Jobs in May as $135 Billion AI Bet Reshapes Its Workforce

content_preview.title: Meta to Cut 8,000 Jobs in May as $135 Billion AI Bet Reshapes Its Workforce
content_preview.summary: Meta plans to eliminate 10% of its global workforce starting May 20, restructuring around AI-driven productivity as it nearly doubles capital spending to $135 billion.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Meta Platforms announced on April 17 that it will begin cutting approximately 8,000 employees — roughly 10 percent of its 78,865-person global workforce — starting May 20, 2026. The restructuring, which the company frames as a transition to an AI-driven operating model, is the largest single round of job reductions at Meta since its 2022–23 "year of efficiency," when roughly 21,000 positions were eliminated. Additional cuts are expected in the second half of 2026, with the full scop...

editor_notes.content_quality: Re-review (Round 2). 718-word News article on Meta's May 2026 layoffs. All four Round 1 findings have been addressed: Q4 net income now attributed to the official PR Newswire earnings release, human_requested correctly set to false, Sama count kept at 1,108 (bot correctly disputed rounding to ~1,000), and capex $72.2B re-attributed to primary source. Article is well-structured with appropriate financial context, industry ripple effects, and a transparent 'What We Don't Know' section.
editor_notes.source_verification: 5 sources read. (1) The Next Web ✅ — WARN act details confirmed: May 20 notification, May 22/29 terminations, 198 CA positions (124 Burlingame, 74 Sunnyvale), 78,865 workforce, 10% cut, affected divisions (Reality Labs, Facebook social, recruiting, sales, global ops), Meta spokesperson quote confirmed. (2) Fortune ✅ — Zuckerberg quote confirmed verbatim, 50:1 ratio target confirmed, Shmulik quotes ('others will rush to replicate it', 'fat exists in every organization') confirmed, $2-4B/$5-8B projections confirmed. (3) Grey Journal ✅ (returned 403 to bot, manually accessible) — AI pod structure, Alexandr Wang (28, Scale AI CEO), $14.3B 49% stake, 2026 capex $115-135B confirmed. (4) Latestly ✅ — 73,000 tech layoffs confirmed, Amazon 30,000 confirmed, Block ~50% confirmed, Sama 1,108 confirmed (specific figure used by Latestly alongside 'approximately 1,000'), $200B revenue/$60B profit confirmed. (5) PR Newswire ✅ — Q4 net income $22.8B confirmed, full-year revenue $201B (22% YoY) confirmed, FCF $43.6B confirmed, capex $72.2B (rounded from $72.22B) confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified. Bot's disputed findings from Round 1 upheld on independent verification: (a) Sama 1,108 — Latestly does contain the specific '1,108 staffers' figure alongside the approximate; using the more precise figure is editorially correct. (b) Capex $72.2B — prnewswire confirms $72.22B; rounding to $72.2B is accurate. (c) Q4 net income $22.8B — confirmed in prnewswire as $22,768M. No hallucinations or misattributions found.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and factual. Shmulik's skeptical framing ('convenient cover for cost-cutting') is appropriately included and attributed. No editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald Meta layoffs coverage. Original and timely.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Approve. All Round 1 issues resolved. Five sources verified with all key claims confirmed. The bot correctly disputed two reviewer findings and independently verified them with primary sources.
